debug_info.goal:
α : Type u_1
β : Type u_2
G✝ H C : Graph α β
S X Y : Set α
M M' : Set β
u v x y z : α
e f : β
P P' : WList α β
hM : @Graph.IsMatching α β G✝ M
G : Graph α β
⊢ ∀ ⦃I B : Set α⦄,
    (∃ M, @Graph.IsMatching α β G M ∧ I ⊆ V(G, M)) →
      ¬Maximal (fun S => ∃ M, @Graph.IsMatching α β G M ∧ S ⊆ V(G, M)) I →
        Maximal (fun S => ∃ M, @Graph.IsMatching α β G M ∧ S ⊆ V(G, M)) B →
          ∃ x ∈ B \ I, ∃ M, @Graph.IsMatching α β G M ∧ insert x I ⊆ V(G, M)

failed_attempts:
intro I B hI hI_not_max hB_max
    have h_main : ∃ x ∈ B \ I, ∃ M, G.IsMatching M ∧ insert x I ⊆ V(G, M) := by
      by_contra! h
      -- We derive a contradiction by showing that the maximal set B cannot exist under the given conditions.
      have h₁ : ∀ (x : α), x ∈ B \ I → ∀ (M : Set β), G.IsMatching M → ¬insert x I ⊆ V(G, M) := by
        intro x hx M hM
        have h₂ := h x hx
        intro h₃
        rcases h₂ with ⟨M', hM', h₄⟩
        <;> aesop
      -- We use the fact that B is maximal to find an element x in B \ I that contradicts the assumption.
      have h₂ : Maximal (fun S => ∃ M, G.IsMatching M ∧ S ⊆ V(G, M)) B := hB_max
      have h₃ : ∀ (x : α), x ∈ B \ I → ∀ (M : Set β), G.IsMatching M → ¬insert x I ⊆ V(G, M) := h₁
      -- We use the fact that I is not maximal to find a contradiction.
      have h₄ : ¬Maximal (fun S => ∃ M, G.IsMatching M ∧ S ⊆ V(G, M)) I := hI_not_max
      -- We use the fact that B is maximal to find an element x in B \ I that contradicts the assumption.
      have h₅ : ∃ (x : α), x ∈ B \ I := by
        by_contra h₅
        -- If B \ I is empty, then B ⊆ I, which contradicts the maximality of B.
        have h₆ : B \ I = ∅ := by
          apply Set.eq_empty_of_forall_not_mem
          intro x hx
          have h₇ := h₅
          exfalso
          exact h₇ ⟨x, hx⟩
        have h₇ : B ⊆ I := by
          have h₈ : B \ I = ∅ := h₆
          have h₉ : B ⊆ I := by
            rw [Set.ext_iff] at h₈
            intro x hx
            by_cases h₁₀ : x ∈ I
            · exact h₁₀
            · have h₁₁ := h₈ x
              simp_all [Set.mem_diff]
              <;> aesop
          exact h₉
        have h₈ : (∃ M, G.IsMatching M ∧ I ⊆ V(G, M)) := hI
        have h₉ : (∃ M, G.IsMatching M ∧ B ⊆ V(G, M)) := by
          obtain ⟨M, hM, hI⟩ := hI
          refine' ⟨M, hM, _⟩
          exact Set.Subset.trans h₇ hI
        have h₁₀ : Maximal (fun S => ∃ M, G.IsMatching M ∧ S ⊆ V(G, M)) I := by
          -- Since B is maximal and B ⊆ I, I must also be maximal.
          have h₁₁ : ∀ (S : Set α), (∃ M, G.IsMatching M ∧ S ⊆ V(G, M)) → I ⊆ S → S = I := by
            intro S hS hIS
            by_contra h₁₂
            have h₁₃ : S ≠ I := h₁₂
            have h₁₄ : S ⊈ I := by
              intro h₁₅
              have h₁₆ : S = I := by
                apply Set.Subset.antisymm
                · exact h₁₅
                · exact hIS
              contradiction
            have h₁₅ : ∃ (x : α), x ∈ S ∧ x ∉ I := by
              by_contra h₁₅
              have h₁₆ : S ⊆ I := by
                intro x hx
                by_cases h₁₇ : x ∈ I
                · exact h₁₇
                · exfalso
                  have h₁₈ := h₁₅
                  push_neg at h₁₈
                  have h₁₉ := h₁₈ x
                  simp_all
                  <;> aesop
              have h₁₉ : S = I := by
                apply Set.Subset.antisymm
                · exact h₁₆
                · exact hIS
              contradiction
            obtain ⟨x, hxS, hxi⟩ := h₁₅
            have h₁₆ : x ∈ S := hxS
            have h₁₇ : x ∉ I := hxi
            have h₁₈ : B ⊆ I := h₇
            have h₁₉ : x ∈ B := by
              by_contra h₂₀
              have h₂₁ : x ∉ B := h₂₀
              have h₂₂ : x ∈ S := hxS
              have h₂₃ : x ∉ I := hxi
              have h₂₄ : ∃ M, G.IsMatching M ∧ S ⊆ V(G, M) := hS
              have h₂₅ : ∃ M, G.IsMatching M ∧ B ⊆ V(G, M) := h₉
              aesop
            have h₂₀ : x ∈ B := h₁₉
            have h₂₁ : x ∈ I := by
              have h₂₂ : B ⊆ I := h₇
              exact h₂₂ h₂₀
            contradiction
          -- Using the above, we can conclude that I is maximal.
          exact
            ⟨hI,
              fun S hS hIS =>
                by_cases hS' : S = I
                · simp_all
                · exfalso
                  have h₁₁ := h₁₁ S hS hIS
                  aesop⟩
        -- Contradiction arises as I is not maximal.
        contradiction
      -- We have an element x in B \ I, and we can use it to find a contradiction.
      obtain ⟨x, hx⟩ := h₅
      have h₆ : x ∈ B \ I := hx
      have h₇ : ∀ (M : Set β), G.IsMatching M → ¬insert x I ⊆ V(G, M) := h₃ x h₆
      -- We use the fact that B is maximal to find a contradiction.
      have h₈ : ∃ (M : Set β), G.IsMatching M ∧ B ⊆ V(G, M) := by
        have h₉ : Maximal (fun S => ∃ M, G.IsMatching M ∧ S ⊆ V(G, M)) B := hB_max
        have h₁₀ : (∃ M, G.IsMatching M ∧ B ⊆ V(G, M)) := by
          exact h₉.1
        exact h₁₀
      obtain ⟨M, hM, hB⟩ := h₈
      have h₉ : ¬insert x I ⊆ V(G, M) := h₇ M hM
      have h₁₀ : insert x I ⊆ V(G, M) := by
        have h₁₁ : I ⊆ V(G, M) := by
          have h₁₂ : (∃ M, G.IsMatching M ∧ I ⊆ V(G, M)) := hI
          have h₁₃ : B ⊆ V(G, M) := hB
          have h₁₄ : I ⊆ B := by
            by_contra h₁₄
            have h₁₅ : ¬I ⊆ B := h₁₄
            have h₁₆ : ∃ (y : α), y ∈ I ∧ y ∉ B := by
              by_contra h₁₆
              have h₁₇ : I ⊆ B := by
                intro y hy
                by_cases h₁₈ : y ∈ B
                · exact h₁₈
                · exfalso
                  have h₁₉ := h₁₆
                  push_neg at h₁₉
                  have h₂₀ := h₁₉ y
                  simp_all
                  <;> aesop
              contradiction
            obtain ⟨y, hyI, hyB⟩ := h₁₆
            have h₁₇ : y ∈ I := hyI
            have h₁₈ : y ∉ B := hyB
            have h₁₉ : (∃ M, G.IsMatching M ∧ B ⊆ V(G, M)) := ⟨M, hM, hB⟩
            have h₂₀ : (∃ M, G.IsMatching M ∧ I ⊆ V(G, M)) := hI
            have h₂₁ : Maximal (fun S => ∃ M, G.IsMatching M ∧ S ⊆ V(G, M)) B := hB_max
            have h₂₂ : ¬Maximal (fun S => ∃ M, G.IsMatching M ∧ S ⊆ V(G, M)) I := hI_not_max
            aesop
          exact Set.Subset.trans h₁₄ hB
        have h₁₂ : x ∈ V(G, M) := by
          have h₁₃ : x ∈ B := by
            have h₁₄ : x ∈ B \ I := hx
            exact h₁₄.1
          have h₁₄ : B ⊆ V(G, M) := hB
          exact h₁₄ h₁₃
        have h₁₃ : insert x I ⊆ V(G, M) := by
          intro y hy
          simp only [Set.mem_insert_iff] at hy
          cases hy with
          | inl hy =>
            exact h₁₂
          | inr hy =>
            have h₁₄ : y ∈ I := hy
            have h₁₅ : I ⊆ V(G, M) := h₁₁
            exact h₁₅ h₁₄
        exact h₁₃
      contradiction
    obtain ⟨x, hx, M, hM, h_insert⟩ := h_main
    refine' ⟨x, hx, M, hM, _⟩
    exact h_insert

intro I B hI hI_not_max hB_max
    -- Use the Maxing Pairs Theorem to find the required element x.
    -- If x is in B but not in I, then we can use it to extend I to I ∪ {x} while maintaining independence.
    classical
    by_contra! h
    -- If no such x exists, then the sets I and B are not as required.
    have h₁ : B ⊆ I := by
      intro x hx
      by_cases hxI : x ∈ I
      · exact hxI
      · exfalso
        have h₂ := h x
        have h₃ : x ∈ B := hx
        have h₄ : x ∉ I := hxI
        have h₅ : x ∈ B \ I := by
          simp_all [Set.mem_diff]
          <;> aesop
        have h₆ := h₂ ⟨h₃, h₄⟩
        simp_all [Set.mem_diff]
        <;> aesop
    -- Since I is not maximal, there exists a larger set that includes I.
    -- But B is maximal, so it cannot be a subset of I unless I is also maximal, which it is not.
    have h₂ : Maximal (fun S => ∃ M, @Graph.IsMatching α β G M ∧ S ⊆ V(G, M)) I := by
      refine' ⟨fun J hJ hIj => _, hI⟩
      -- If there is a set J that includes I, then we need to check if J is matchable.
      -- However, since I is not maximal, this leads to a contradiction.
      have h₃ : I ⊆ J := hIj
      have h₄ : ∃ M, @Graph.IsMatching α β G M ∧ J ⊆ V(G, M) := hJ
      -- Since B is maximal and I is not, we can use the fact that B is maximal to find a contradiction.
      have h₅ : I ⊆ B := by
        intro x hx
        -- If x is in I, then it cannot be in B \ I, so it must be in B.
        by_contra h₆
        have h₇ : x ∈ I := hx
        have h₈ : x ∉ B := h₆
        have h₉ : x ∈ B \ I := by
          simp_all [Set.mem_diff]
          <;> aesop
        have h₁₀ := h x
        have h₁₁ : x ∈ B := by
          simp_all [Set.mem_diff]
          <;> aesop
        have h₁₂ : x ∉ I := by
          simp_all [Set.mem_diff]
          <;> aesop
        have h₁₃ := h₁₀ ⟨h₁₁, h₁₂⟩
        simp_all [Set.mem_diff]
        <;> aesop
      -- Since I ⊆ B and J includes I, we can check if J includes B.
      -- If not, this leads to a contradiction with B being maximal.
      by_cases h₆ : J = B
      · simp_all [h₆]
      · exfalso
        have h₇ : J ⊈ B := by
          intro h₈
          have h₉ : J ⊆ B := h₈
          have h₁₀ : J ≠ B := h₆
          simp_all [Set.ssubset_iff_of_subset]
          <;> aesop
        have h₈ : ∃ M, @Graph.IsMatching α β G M ∧ J ⊆ V(G, M) := hJ
        have h₉ : Maximal (fun S => ∃ M, @Graph.IsMatching α β G M ∧ S ⊆ V(G, M)) B := hB_max
        have h₁₀ := h₉.1 J h₈
        have h₁₁ : J ⊆ B := by
          simp_all [Set.ssubset_iff_of_subset]
          <;> aesop
        simp_all [Set.ssubset_iff_of_subset]
        <;> aesop
    -- Contradiction arises as I was assumed not to be maximal.
    simp_all [not_forall, not_not]
    <;> aesop

intro I B hI hI_not_max hB_max
    have h₁ : ∃ x ∈ B \ I, ∃ M, G.IsMatching M ∧ insert x I ⊆ V(G, M) := by
      -- Assume that no such x exists and derive a contradiction.
      by_contra! h
      -- If no such x exists, then for every x in B \ I, no matching contains x and all of I.
      have h₂ : ∀ x ∈ B \ I, ∀ M : Set β, ¬(G.IsMatching M ∧ insert x I ⊆ V(G, M)) := by
        intro x hx M
        by_cases hx' : x ∈ B \ I
        · -- If x is in B \ I, use the assumption h to get a contradiction.
          have h₃ := h x hx'
          by_contra h₄
          aesop
        · -- If x is not in B \ I, this case is vacuously true.
          simp_all
      -- Use the given maximality of B to derive a contradiction.
      have h₃ : Maximal (fun S => ∃ M, G.IsMatching M ∧ S ⊆ V(G, M)) B := hB_max
      have h₄ : ¬Maximal (fun S => ∃ M, G.IsMatching M ∧ S ⊆ V(G, M)) I := hI_not_max
      -- Find an element in B \ I that can be added to I to form a larger independent set.
      have h₅ : ∃ x ∈ B \ I, True := by
        by_contra! h₅
        have h₆ : B \ I = ∅ := by
          apply Set.eq_empty_of_forall_not_mem
          intro x hx
          have h₇ := h₅ x
          aesop
        have h₇ : B ⊆ I := by
          have h₈ : B \ I = ∅ := h₆
          have h₉ : B ⊆ I := by
            intro x hx
            by_cases hx' : x ∈ I
            · exact hx'
            · have h₁₀ : x ∈ B \ I := by
                exact ⟨hx, hx'⟩
              have h₁₁ : x ∉ B \ I := by
                rw [h₈]
                simp
              contradiction
          exact h₉
        -- Use the maximality of B to get a contradiction.
        have h₈ : (fun S => ∃ M, G.IsMatching M ∧ S ⊆ V(G, M)) B := by
          have h₉ : Maximal (fun S => ∃ M, G.IsMatching M ∧ S ⊆ V(G, M)) B := hB_max
          exact h₉.1
        have h₉ : (fun S => ∃ M, G.IsMatching M ∧ S ⊆ V(G, M)) I := by
          obtain ⟨M, hM, hM'⟩ := hI
          exact ⟨M, hM, hM'⟩
        have h₁₀ : B ⊆ I := h₇
        have h₁₁ : ∃ M, G.IsMatching M ∧ I ⊆ V(G, M) := hI
        have h₁₂ : Maximal (fun S => ∃ M, G.IsMatching M ∧ S ⊆ V(G, M)) B := hB_max
        -- Use the fact that I is not maximal to get a contradiction.
        have h₁₃ : ¬Maximal (fun S => ∃ M, G.IsMatching M ∧ S ⊆ V(G, M)) I := hI_not_max
        -- Use the fact that B is maximal to get a contradiction.
        have h₁₄ : Maximal (fun S => ∃ M, G.IsMatching M ∧ S ⊆ V(G, M)) B := hB_max
        -- Use the fact that B is maximal to get a contradiction.
        simp_all [Set.Subset.antisymm_iff]
        <;> aesop
      obtain ⟨x, hx, _⟩ := h₅
      have h₆ := h₂ x hx ∅
      have h₇ : ¬(G.IsMatching ∅ ∧ insert x I ⊆ V(G, ∅)) := h₆
      have h₈ : G.IsMatching ∅ := by
        exact ⟨by simp, by simp⟩
      have h₉ : insert x I ⊆ V(G, ∅) → False := by
        intro h₉
        have h₁₀ : G.IsMatching ∅ ∧ insert x I ⊆ V(G, ∅) := ⟨h₈, h₉⟩
        exact h₇ h₁₀
      have h₁₀ : insert x I ⊆ V(G, ∅) → False := h₉
      have h₁₁ : ¬(insert x I ⊆ V(G, ∅)) := by
        intro h₁₁
        exact h₁₀ h₁₁
      have h₁₂ : insert x I ⊆ V(G, ∅) → False := h₁₀
      simp [V_empty, Set.subset_def] at h₁₁ h₁₂ ⊢
      <;>
      (try simp_all) <;>
      (try aesop) <;>
      (try
        {
          have h₁₃ := h₁₁
          simp_all [Set.subset_def]
          <;>
          aesop
        }) <;>
      (try
        {
          have h₁₃ := h₁₁
          simp_all [Set.subset_def]
          <;>
          aesop
        })
    obtain ⟨x, hx, M, hM, hM'⟩ := h₁
    refine' ⟨x, hx, M, hM, _⟩
    <;> aesop

intro I B hI hI' hB
    have h₁ : ¬ Maximal (fun S => ∃ M, G.IsMatching M ∧ S ⊆ V(G, M)) I := hI'
    have h₂ : Maximal (fun S => ∃ M, G.IsMatching M ∧ S ⊆ V(G, M)) B := hB
    classical
    by_cases hB' : ∃ x ∈ B \ I, ∃ M, G.IsMatching M ∧ (insert x I ⊆ V(G, M))
    · obtain ⟨x, hx, M, hM, hM'⟩ := hB'
      exact ⟨x, hx, ⟨M, hM, hM'⟩⟩
    · exfalso
      -- We will show that if no such x exists, then we get a contradiction with the maximality of I.
      have h₃ : ∀ x ∈ B \ I, ∀ M, G.IsMatching M → ¬ (insert x I ⊆ V(G, M)) := by
        intro x hx M hM
        by_contra h₄
        have h₅ : ∃ x ∈ B \ I, ∃ M, G.IsMatching M ∧ (insert x I ⊆ V(G, M)) := ⟨x, hx, M, hM, h₄⟩
        contradiction
      -- We use the fact that B is a maximal independent set to derive a contradiction.
      have h₄ : ∃ M, G.IsMatching M ∧ B ⊆ V(G, M) := by
        apply hB
      obtain ⟨M, hM, hM'⟩ := h₄
      -- Since I is not maximal, there exists a larger independent set.
      have h₅ : ∃ S, (fun S => ∃ M, G.IsMatching M ∧ S ⊆ V(G, M)) S ∧ ¬ S ⊆ I := by
        by_contra h₅
        push_neg at h₅
        have h₆ : ∀ S, (fun S => ∃ M, G.IsMatching M ∧ S ⊆ V(G, M)) S → S ⊆ I := by
          intro S hS
          have h₇ : ¬ ¬ (S ⊆ I) := by
            intro h₈
            have h₉ : ¬ S ⊆ I := h₈
            have h₁₀ : ∃ S, (fun S => ∃ M, G.IsMatching M ∧ S ⊆ V(G, M)) S ∧ ¬ S ⊆ I := ⟨S, hS, h₉⟩
            contradiction
          exact not_not.mp h₇
        have h₇ : Maximal (fun S => ∃ M, G.IsMatching M ∧ S ⊆ V(G, M)) I := by
          refine' ⟨hI, _⟩
          intro S hS
          exact h₆ S hS
        contradiction
      obtain ⟨S, hS, hS'⟩ := h₅
      obtain ⟨M', hM', hS''⟩ := hS
      have h₆ : S ⊆ V(G, M') := hS''
      -- We derive a contradiction by showing that S is not contained in I.
      have h₇ : ¬ S ⊆ I := hS'
      -- This leads to a contradiction because B is maximal.
      have h₈ : False := by
        by_contra h₈
        -- Use the fact that B is maximal to get a contradiction.
        have h₉ : ∃ x ∈ B \ I, ∃ M, G.IsMatching M ∧ (insert x I ⊆ V(G, M)) := by
          sorry
        contradiction
      contradiction
